label: Lacrymara
description: Lacrymara is a temperate, high-albedo planet whose silicate plains are glazed by periodic fallouts of electrostatically charged glass dust from Vesperglass System’s vitrified belt. A shallow global sea, rich in suspended reflective particulates, throws the copper-white twilight into broad bands that preserve the system’s mirror-halo effect even at low altitude. The surface topography is dominated by fault-limned basins and long escarpments that catch radiance currents as steady, luminous seam-lines. Orbital observations record a persistent, faint auroral girdle aligned with the system’s heliopause geometry, giving the planet a fixed, navigationally useful signature within the hushlit lattice.
